summary: Use this switch to control command argument partial matching as well as enum value partial matching. When it's turned on, argument '-opt' will match '-option' if no other argument name begins with -opt. Default is 'quiet', which means to silently allow partial-matching when there's no ambiguity. 'warn' means allow partial-matching with a warning message. 'error' means to disable partial-matching, and error out.
description: Use this switch to control command argument partial matching as well as enum value partial matching. When it's turned on, argument '-opt' will match '-option' if no other argument name begins with -opt. Default is 'quiet', which means to silently allow partial-matching when there's no ambiguity. 'warn' means allow partial-matching with a warning message. 'error' means to disable partial-matching, and error out.
